diagnosis: Elsevier authors and abstract classifier found the remaining current-Goldie gap is mixed and mostly not broad-parser owned. Authors have 23 parser_miss rows but 16 gold-owned and 15 no-action/unclear rows; abstract has 19 parser-owned rows versus 89 gold-owned/format-diff rows plus 8 retrieval errors. Broad parser edits would be low-yield and risky without tighter DOI-template lanes.

authors.next_action: No broad authors parser patch from this classifier. Send 16 fix_gold and 4 unclear rows to gold-auditor; investigate the 23 parser_miss rows only through a title-byline/portal manual-DOM specialist lane.

policy: Current-Goldie KPI stays separate from Goldie-backfilled. Gold-owned rows are not parser wins; parser rows need DOI-template evidence and Shield gates before code changes.
recommendation: Keep sprint active but move Elsevier authors/abstract out of generic needs_agent. Next highest safe code candidate is a narrow Elsevier citation_abstract short-meta fallback; remaining title-byline and portal cases are manual-DOM or gold-auditor lanes.
